intentional_test_features: Over-triage avoidance / reassurance: atypical sharp brief left-sided chest pain with palpitations and health anxiety in a young adult with benign mitral valve prolapse - the AI Doctor should reassure appropriately and avoid an unnecessary overnight ED presentation
Non-anginal pain pattern: sharp, brief, localised, non-exertional pain (not crushing, not exertional, no arm/jaw radiation) argues against acute coronary syndrome
Benign structural variant: a mid-systolic click with a late systolic murmur that becomes dynamic on standing is characteristic of MVP - a common, generally benign condition
Health-anxiety management: the presentation is driven by MVP-related somatic symptoms amplified by anxiety; the AI Doctor must reassure without dismissing, and offer lifestyle measures
Retained safety-netting: genuine ACS red flags (central crushing chest pain >15 min) and syncope must still be safety-netted despite the benign picture

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
